title: 2026 Youth Innovation Challenge: Win $1,000 and a Fully Funded Trip to Taiwan for Marine Conservation Innovators
summary: Young changemakers passionate about ocean conservation now have a global opportunity to make an impact. The 2026 Youth Innovation Challenge (YIC), organized by Taiwan’s Ocean Conservation Administration (OCA) in partnership with the Global Environmental Education Partnership (GEEP), is officially open for applications. This exciting international competition invites young people aged 15 to 30 from around the world to submit innovative solutions that address pressing marine environmental challenges. Participants are encouraged to create practical, research-based, and measurable ideas that help protect marine ecosystems, improve ocean health, and inspire communities to become active stewards of the environment. Since 2021, the OCA has collaborated with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (U.S. EPA) and the North American Association for Environmental Education (NAAEE) through GEEP to empower youth leaders globally. Now in its sixth year, the challenge continues to inspire the next generation of environmental innovators. About the 2026 Youth Innovation Challenge The 2026 edition of the Youth Innovation Challenge focuses on promoting a holistic approach to ocean conservation. This year’s theme encourages participants to begin with individual action and scale their efforts to families, communities, and even international advocacy. The goal is to transform creative ideas into real-world action by using environmental education as a tool for lasting change. Applicants must propose solutions that are: Informed by research Feasible and measurable Innovative and practical Focused on one of the challenge’s key marine conservation topics The challenge seeks solutions that contribute to healthier marine habitats, protect human health, and strengthen environmental awareness among people of all ages. Key Focus Areas for 2026 This year’s challenge centers on three important themes: 1. Reducing Personal Waste and Marine Debris Participants can propose ways to reduce plastic pollution, personal waste, and harmful debris entering oceans and waterways. 2. Promoting Positive Community and Societal Impact Ideas should encourage sustainable habits and environmental responsibility within local communities and broader society. 3. Advocating International Marine Environmental Education Applicants can also focus on educational programs, awareness campaigns, and global partnerships that support marine conservation. Benefits for Selected Winners Three outstanding winners will be selected for the 2026 Youth Innovation Challenge. Each winner will receive: $1,000 USD cash prize Fully funded invitation to Taiwan Opportunity to attend the official award ceremony Chance to present their idea at an international forum Global recognition for their innovation and leadership This is a unique opportunity for young environmental leaders to gain international exposure and connect with experts in marine conservation. Who Can Apply? The competition is open to: Young people aged 15 to 30 years old Applicants from any country around the world Individuals or teams with innovative marine conservation ideas Please note that all submissions must be completed in English. Application Deadline The deadline to submit proposals is: May 31, 2026 at 11:59 PM U.S. Eastern Time Interested applicants are encouraged to start early and carefully review the submission guidelines. How to Apply To apply successfully, applicants should follow these steps: Step 1: Read the Applicant Handbook Review the Youth Innovation Challenge Applicant Handbook to understand the eligibility requirements, judging criteria, and submission process. Step 2: Register for the Applicant Webinar NAAEE will host a live applicant webinar on: May 5, 2026 10:00 PM (Taiwan Time) The webinar will provide proposal writing tips, guidance for strong applications, and a live Q A session. Step 3: Submit Your Proposal Complete and submit your proposal before the deadline through the official application portal. APPLY HERE The ocean is one of humanity’s greatest shared treasures, and protecting it requires collective action from all generations. The 2026 Youth Innovation Challenge provides a powerful platform for young innovators to transform bold ideas into meaningful environmental change. Whether your passion lies in reducing plastic waste, educating communities, or creating international awareness, this challenge gives you the chance to turn your vision into action. If you have a solution that can help protect our oceans, now is the time to share it with the world. Discover similar opportunities Here. The post 2026 Youth Innovation Challenge: Win $1,000 and a Fully Funded Trip to Taiwan for Marine Conservation Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Docusign Foundation Agreements for Good Grants 2026: Funding Global Collaborations for Lasting Impact
summary: The Docusign Foundation has launched its impactful Agreements for Good program—an initiative designed to fund collaborations that build trust, strengthen partnerships, and create long-term solutions to some of the world’s most pressing challenges. Rooted in the broader mission of DocuSign —to redefine how the world comes together and agrees—this program goes beyond traditional funding. It invests in how people collaborate , ensuring that agreements are not only made but sustained for meaningful, lasting change. About the Agreements for Good Program The Agreements for Good initiative focuses on funding collaboration itself —supporting systems, partnerships, and shared accountability structures that enable bold ideas to thrive. Rather than only financing outcomes, the program emphasizes: Trust-building processes Alignment between stakeholders Long-term collaboration frameworks This approach ensures that projects are not just successful in the short term, but resilient and scalable over time . Funding Structure The Docusign Foundation offers two types of funding support: 1. One-Time Grants Designed to launch new partnerships and collaborations Ideal for pilot initiatives or early-stage coalitions 2. Multi-Year Grants Designed to sustain and strengthen established collaborations Supports long-term impact and continuity Priority Areas The foundation invests in three critical areas where trust and agreements are essential: 1. Environment Sustainability Supports initiatives that promote harmony between people and nature Focuses on building a resilient and sustainable planet 2. Data Privacy Security Funds efforts that protect digital identities and privacy Encourages trust in emerging technologies, including AI 3. Future of Work Advances solutions that ensure economic stability and opportunity Promotes dignity and inclusion in a rapidly evolving workforce What the Program Funds The Agreements for Good program supports: Collaborations and partnerships aligned with the three priority areas Development of: Governance models Collaborative infrastructure Enforceable agreements Activities such as: Convenings and stakeholder meetings Facilitation and coordination Agreement design and structuring Prototype partnerships Backbone organization support Eligibility Criteria To qualify for funding, applicants must meet the following requirements: Collaboration Requirements One-time grants: Minimum of two organizations working together Multi-year grants: Minimum of three organizations in formal collaboration Organizational Requirements The lead applicant must be an eligible entity such as: Registered nonprofits NGOs Charities 501(c)(3) organizations or global equivalents Fiscally sponsored projects Government entities (state, tribal, or local) may apply as the backbone organization Preferred Collaboration Model Cross-sector partnerships are strongly encouraged Example: nonprofit + private company + public agency Geographic Scope Applications are accepted globally Projects can operate at: Local level Regional level Global level The foundation supports initiatives both in regions where Docusign operates and anywhere in the world aligned with its mission. What Is Not Funded The program does not support : Projects outside the three priority areas Individual applicants For-profit businesses Religious or discriminatory organizations Political campaigns or election-related activities Fundraising events Projects lacking a collaborative or agreement-building component Click HERE to start the application process. Why This Grant Matters This opportunity is unique because it shifts the focus from simply funding projects to funding the relationships and agreements that make projects succeed . By investing in: Trust Collaboration Shared accountability The Docusign Foundation ensures that funded initiatives can endure, adapt, and scale over time. How to Learn More Applicants are encouraged to review the full application FAQs and explore the foundation’s annual Impact Report to understand past successes and funding priorities in depth. Click HERE to review FAQs. Discover more Grant opportunities on OFY HERE The post Docusign Foundation Agreements for Good Grants 2026: Funding Global Collaborations for Lasting Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Japanese Award (MIDP) 2026: $125,000 Grant for NGOs Driving Innovation in Peace and Human Security
summary: The 2026 edition of the MIDP Awards presents a powerful opportunity for non-profit organizations to secure funding and scale impactful solutions that address some of the world’s most pressing challenges. With a strong focus on Science, Technology, and Innovation (STI) for Peace and Human Security , this global call invites NGOs and Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) to submit transformative projects that are already in the implementation phase and demonstrate high potential for impact. This prestigious award program is designed to uplift initiatives that directly serve marginalized and disadvantaged communities in developing nations, especially those recognized as low- and middle-income countries by the World Bank. About the MIDP Awards 2026 The MIDP Awards recognize and support projects that demonstrate innovation, scalability, and measurable impact . The program prioritizes solutions that improve service delivery and address real-world challenges faced by vulnerable populations. Key Focus Areas Projects submitted under this call should: Address urgent needs of underserved communities Improve livelihoods Deliver rapid and tangible results Promote sustainable development Enable scalability and long-term impact Strengthen systems and infrastructure Leverage innovation Use science, technology, and creative approaches Introduce new models or improve existing systems Funding and Award Structure A total of US$125,000 in grants will be awarded to the most outstanding projects: Two First Prize Winners Each receives US$50,000 Second Prize Winner Receives US$25,000 Additional Benefits Beyond funding, awardees will receive: Dedicated Technical Advisor Guidance throughout the entire implementation period Global Recognition Visibility among international development stakeholders Opportunity for Scale-Up Pathway to larger funding mechanisms Japan Social Development Fund (JSDF) Award Top-performing projects from the MIDP Awards will have an opportunity to compete for the Japan Social Development Fund (JSDF) Award . What Makes JSDF Unique? Targets community-driven development initiatives Focuses on: Poverty reduction Improved access to services and infrastructure Increased productivity Funding Opportunity Eligible projects may receive up to US$200,000 Selection occurs after at least 12 months of successful MIDP grant implementation Projects are assessed for replicability and large-scale impact Connector Grant: Expanding Global Collaboration Introduced in 2022, the Connector Grant is designed to foster international collaboration and evidence-based scaling. Purpose of the Connector Grant Support partnerships between: Research institutions (ORD grantees) MIDP and JSDF award recipients Strengthen: Monitoring and Evaluation (M E) Impact measurement systems Key Outcomes Enhanced project credibility through research-backed insights Improved scalability through collaborative frameworks Stronger global development networks Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for: Non-profit NGOs Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) Project Requirements Eligible projects must: Already be in the implementation phase Demonstrate high potential for impact Target marginalized and vulnerable populations Be located in developing countries What Makes a Winning Proposal? Projects that stand out typically: Offer innovative and practical solutions Show clear evidence of impact Address real community needs Have strong potential for scaling up Include measurable outcomes and sustainability plans Application Resources Applicants are encouraged to review all supporting materials before submission. Click to open the supporting resources for your review. They include: MIDP Project Call and Guidelines Application Template Indicators Template FAQs Documents required for shortlisted applications Call for MIDP Reviewers These resources provide detailed insights into: Eligibility criteria Review process Selection methodology Application procedures For more information about this opportunity, click HERE Deadline The deadline for submission of applications is: June 17, 2026 (6:00 PM IST) Late submissions will not be considered, so applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare and submit their proposals well in advance. Grievance and Support For any concerns regarding the implementation of the program or its outcomes, applicants may reach out via: Email: awards@gdn.int Copy: fobino@gdn.int Additional guidance is available through the official GDN website. Why You Should Apply This is more than just a funding opportunity—it is a chance to: Transform lives in underserved communities Scale impactful innovations globally Collaborate with international experts Gain recognition and long-term support If your organization is already driving change and looking to expand its reach, the MIDP Awards 2026 could be the catalyst you need. title: Loughborough University IAS Residential Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded UK Research Fellowship for International Scholars
summary: The Institute of Advanced Studies (IAS) at Loughborough University is inviting applications for its prestigious IAS Residential Fellowships 2026 , offering outstanding international researchers the opportunity to undertake a fully funded one-month research residency in the United Kingdom. This fellowship is designed for exceptional scholars across all disciplines and career stages who wish to engage with the vibrant academic community at Loughborough University and contribute to innovative interdisciplinary research collaborations. The programme supports international scholars, artists, writers, public intellectuals, and policy-makers whose work aligns with Loughborough University’s areas of expertise and research excellence. About the IAS Residential Fellowship The IAS Residential Fellowship programme aims to strengthen global academic partnerships, encourage interdisciplinary collaboration, and create long-term research connections between international scholars and Loughborough University. Applicants are expected to propose a research project that will contribute to new and emerging areas of scholarship while building meaningful collaboration with Loughborough researchers. To support this, applicants must either: Nominate an academic colleague or research group at Loughborough University they wish to collaborate with, or Identify an IAS Research Summit taking place during the academic year that aligns with their research interests The fellowship particularly encourages proposals that include: Joint publications Collaborative research grant applications Future interdisciplinary research projects Long-term international scholarly partnerships Please note that this fellowship is not open to applicants currently undertaking doctoral research (PhD students). Who Can Apply? Applications are welcome from: Established academics Early-career researchers Independent scholars Artists and writers Public intellectuals Policy-makers outside academia Applicants from all disciplines and countries are encouraged to apply, especially those whose work promotes: Interdisciplinary knowledge Intercultural understanding Social responsibility Equality, diversity, and inclusion Fellowship Benefits Successful IAS Residential Fellows will receive: Fully Funded Travel Support Return travel to Loughborough, UK Visa application support where required Accommodation One-bedroom accommodation at the IAS Flat located in the heart of Loughborough’s Midlands campus, or Two-bedroom accommodation at the IAS House located just outside the campus Applicants travelling with family members should indicate this in their application. £1,500 Fixed Stipend A stipend of £1,500 will be provided to cover: Subsistence Local travel Incidental living costs during the residency Research Support and University Access Fellows will also receive: Flexible working space at International House (home of the IAS) Full access to the University Library Possible access to specialist research equipment and facilities (case-by-case basis) Formal and informal networking opportunities across the university Support for seminars, workshops, roundtables, and collaborative events One-to-one funding opportunities session with Loughborough University Responsibilities of Fellows IAS Residential Fellows are expected to: Be physically present at Loughborough University during the fellowship period Participate actively in IAS events and activities Engage with staff and postgraduate researchers Deliver at least: One work-in-progress seminar One workshop for doctoral researchers Attendance at IAS Friends and Fellows Coffee Mornings Submit a short report within 3 months after the fellowship ends Acknowledge IAS support in resulting publications Join the Loughborough IAS Alumni community Fellowship Duration The fellowship lasts for one calendar month and can be taken anytime between August and July of the academic year, depending on agreement between the Fellow and the IAS. Applicants will indicate their preferred month during the application process. Most fellows will be based on the main Loughborough campus, although where research links to Loughborough’s London campus, the IAS can facilitate stays across both campuses. Longer or shorter fellowship periods may be considered on a case-by-case basis. ATAS Approval Requirement Applicants should also check whether they require ATAS (Academic Technology Approval Scheme) clearance before traveling. ATAS approval is required for certain subject areas—mainly in science, engineering, and technology—where research knowledge could potentially relate to weapons development. Applicants are advised to review the UK government’s ATAS requirements before applying. Application Deadline This is an annual call that opens every January and closes in May. Upcoming Deadlines 5:00 PM, Friday 15 May 2026 — for visits between September 2026 and July 2027 5:00 PM, Friday 21 May 2027 — for visits between September 2027 and July 2028 5:00 PM, Friday 19 May 2028 — for visits between September 2028 and July 2029 How to Apply The IAS Residential Fellowship is highly competitive, with approximately 12 fellowships awarded each academic year . Applicants must submit: Completed IAS Residential Fellowship Application Form CV (maximum 4 pages) Applications should be sent via email to: ias.applications@mailbox.lboro.ac.uk Please note that the application form was updated in July 2025, and all future applicants must use the new version. Final decisions will be announced by July at the end of the academic year. The IAS Residential Fellowship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for international scholars to engage in high-level research, build valuable global collaborations, and experience academic life at one of the UK’s leading universities. With full travel support, accommodation, stipend funding, and access to world-class research networks, this fellowship is ideal for researchers seeking to expand their international academic impact. If you are an ambitious scholar looking to advance your research and establish meaningful partnerships in the UK, this is an opportunity worth pursuing. Discover similar Fellowship opportunities Here. The post Loughborough University IAS Residential Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded UK Research Fellowship for International Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Neurotorium Clinical Education Grants 2026: Secure Up to 100,000 DKK Per Year to Transform Psychiatry and Neurology Education Globally
summary: The Neurotorium Clinical Education Grants 2026 present a powerful opportunity for professionals, educators, and institutions working in psychiatry and neurology to secure funding for impactful, non-profit educational initiatives. Designed to strengthen clinical competencies and improve patient outcomes, this grant supports innovative educational projects that enhance understanding of brain-related disorders worldwide. With funding of up to 100,000 DKK per year for up to three years , this program is ideal for organizations and professionals seeking to advance clinical education in areas such as epilepsy, depression, anxiety, stroke, Parkinson’s disease, and bipolar disorder. About Neurotorium Neurotorium is a global educational platform offering free, high-quality, and unbiased learning materials focused on brain science and mental health. Its resources are developed and continuously updated by leading experts, making it a trusted hub for clinicians, researchers, and educators worldwide. Through its Clinical Education Grants, Neurotorium aims to expand access to cutting-edge knowledge and improve healthcare delivery in psychiatry and neurology. Purpose of the Grant The Neurotorium Clinical Education Grants are designed to: Support non-profit educational projects for healthcare professionals Strengthen clinical skills in prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of neurological and psychiatric disorders Promote the use of Neurotorium’s educational resources in teaching and training Encourage global collaboration and innovation , especially in underserved and low-income regions Eligible Projects To qualify for funding, proposed projects must meet the following criteria: 1. Educational Focus Activities must be at vocational or university level Must target healthcare professionals (e.g., clinicians, nurses, general practitioners, residents) 2. Subject Relevance Projects should aim to improve understanding of: Prevention, diagnosis, and/or treatment of psychiatric and neurological disorders Personal and societal impacts of these conditions 3. Use of Neurotorium Resources Projects must integrate Neurotorium materials partially or fully 4. Non-Profit Requirement All initiatives must be strictly non-commercial 5. Institutional Support Must be anchored in an established organization , such as: Universities Hospitals Clinics NGOs A letter of support is mandatory 6. Duration and Timeline Projects can run for 1 to 3 years Must begin no earlier than February 2027 Who Can Apply? Applicants must: Have relevant professional experience in the proposed field Demonstrate the capacity to execute the project successfully Submit only one application per year as the main applicant There are no geographical restrictions , making this a truly global opportunity. Funding Details Up to 100,000 DKK (~$15,000 USD) per year Funding duration: 1 to 3 years Covers direct project costs only Funds cannot be transferred between years Partial funding is allowed only if other funding sources are secured Application Requirements Applications must be submitted in English and include: Project title and summary (with start and end dates) Detailed project description Explanation of the project’s impact on clinical practice Feasibility analysis Description of how Neurotorium resources will be used List of educators involved Applicant and co-applicant CVs and publications Detailed budget breakdown Letter of support (mandatory) If resubmitting: response to previous reviewer feedback Applications must be submitted through the official LANAS platform, and applicants are encouraged to consult the detailed Applicant Guide before submission. Evaluation Criteria Applications will be assessed by the Neurotorium Editorial Board based on: 1. Innovation and Concept Originality and educational value Quality of the proposed idea 2. Impact Relevance to clinical needs Potential to improve diagnosis, prevention, and treatment Long-term sustainability 3. Feasibility Applicant’s expertise Strength of partnerships Availability of infrastructure 4. Integration of Neurotorium Clear and meaningful use of platform resources Timeline Application Deadline: September 2nd, 2026 Grant Decision: December 2026 Project Start Date: From February 2027 Grant Conditions Successful applicants must: Accept the Lundbeck Foundation’s General Terms and Conditions Ensure funds are managed through a non-commercial organization Past Impact Since 2024, Neurotorium has funded: 18 projects across 17 countries Initiatives ranging from workshops and training sessions to online and hybrid learning programs Projects targeting diverse healthcare providers, including: Psychiatrists and neurologists General practitioners Emergency workers Community health workers These initiatives are collectively improving clinical care and strengthening healthcare systems globally. Contact Information For inquiries regarding eligibility or application requirements, please contact: Bryn Farnsworth von Cederwald – Scientific Programme Manager How to Apply Applications must be submitted through LANAS. Apply here Please note that the use of photos should be avoided or limited within the application, and all applications must be completed in English. Learn More Detailed opportunity: Click here. More grant opportunities for youth: Visit the OFY website: Click here . The post Neurotorium Clinical Education Grants 2026: Secure Up to 100,000 DKK Per Year to Transform Psychiatry and Neurology Education Globally appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Child Survival Call 2026 by ‘la Caixa’ Foundation: Funding Partnerships to Save Lives in Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: The Child Survival Call 2026 launched by the la Caixa Foundation is a high-impact funding opportunity designed to support collaborative projects that aim to reduce child and maternal mortality in sub-Saharan Africa . This initiative aligns with global development priorities and provides a strategic platform for non-profit organizations to work together in tackling one of the most urgent public health challenges. With a strong emphasis on partnerships and measurable impact, this call supports projects that contribute directly to achieving the targets of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Sustainable Development Goal 3 (Good Health and Well-being) and Sustainable Development Goal 17 (Partnerships for the Goals). Overview of the Child Survival Call 2026 The Child Survival Call 2026 focuses on improving healthcare outcomes for newborns, children under five, and mothers in regions where mortality rates remain critically high. Key Highlights: Supports collaborative, multi-partner projects Targets sub-Saharan African countries with high mortality rates Encourages international cooperation and alliances Promotes sustainable, scalable health interventions Objective of the Call The main objective of this funding initiative is to: Reduce preventable deaths of newborns and children under five Improve maternal health outcomes Strengthen health systems and community-level interventions Encourage cross-border collaboration and knowledge sharing This initiative is part of a broader global effort to meet the 2030 targets set under the United Nations SDGs. Geographic Focus: Eligible Countries Projects funded under this call must be implemented in one or more of the following countries: Angola Ethiopia Guinea-Bissau Kenya Mozambique Sierra Leone Tanzania Uganda Each project must include at least one organization based in the country of implementation , ensuring local relevance and sustainability. Who Can Apply? The call is specifically designed for networks of organizations , not individual applicants. Eligibility Criteria: Applications must be submitted by consortia of 3 to 5 entities Eligible entities include: Spanish non-profit organizations Portuguese non-profit organizations International non-profit organizations Each consortium must: Include at least one local partner from the target country Demonstrate strong collaboration and shared objectives Focus on Partnerships and Collaboration A defining feature of this call is its emphasis on multi-stakeholder partnerships . Successful proposals are expected to: Combine diverse expertise and resources Foster long-term cooperation between organizations Ensure local engagement and ownership Deliver integrated and sustainable health solutions Application Process Applications must be submitted through the official “la Caixa” Foundation Call Application platform . Required Steps: Review the Rules for Participation document Consult the Application User Manual Prepare all necessary documentation Submit the application before the deadline Applicants are strongly encouraged to carefully follow all guidelines to ensure eligibility and completeness. Key Dates and Timeline Call Opens: April 21, 2026 (12:00 noon CET) Application Deadline: May 26, 2026 (5:00 pm CET) Information Session: April 20, 2026 (3:30 – 4:30 pm CET) Applicants should plan their submissions carefully to meet all requirements before the deadline. Supporting Resources To assist applicants, the foundation provides: A detailed Rules for Participation document A comprehensive Application User Manual A FAQ section addressing common questions These resources are essential for preparing a strong and compliant application. Impact and Long-Term Vision Through this call, the la Caixa Foundation aims to: Support innovative and evidence-based health interventions Strengthen health systems in vulnerable regions Build sustainable partnerships that extend beyond the project duration Contribute to global health equity and development goals This initiative represents a critical opportunity for organizations committed to improving child and maternal health outcomes in sub-Saharan Africa. Apply here Learn More Detailed opportunity: Click here. More grant opportunities for youth: Visit the OFY website: Click here. The post Child Survival Call 2026 by la Caixa Foundation: Funding Partnerships to Save Lives in Sub-Saharan Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Enlight Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Program for Youth Mental Wellbeing Leaders and Social Entrepreneurs
summary: The Enlight Fellowship 2026 , powered by Watson Institute and supported by the Enlight Foundation, is a prestigious fully funded program designed to empower emerging leaders working to improve youth mental wellbeing worldwide. This highly competitive fellowship equips participants with the entrepreneurial skills, leadership training, mentorship, and global network needed to scale impactful solutions. If you are building a venture focused on mental health and social impact, this fellowship offers a transformative opportunity to accelerate your growth and expand your global influence. About the Enlight Fellowship The Enlight Fellowship is a 16-week leadership and entrepreneurship development program tailored specifically for individuals addressing youth mental wellbeing challenges. It combines immersive learning, expert mentorship, and practical application to help fellows scale their ventures and deepen their impact. Core Objectives of the Fellowship Strengthen leadership capacity among youth-focused innovators Foster cross-cultural collaboration and global partnerships Equip fellows with tools to scale sustainable mental wellbeing solutions Build a long-term global network of changemakers The program begins with a week-long immersive experience , followed by structured virtual sessions, mentorship, and hands-on project implementation. Priority Application Deadline Priority Deadline: July 5, 2026 Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis after this date until all slots are filled Final decisions are announced by July 20, 2026 , and the program officially begins on August 17, 2026 . Program Timeline and Key Dates Program Duration August 17 – December 18, 2026 In-Person Immersive Experience (Choose One Location) Boulder, Colorado Week 1: August 17 – August 21, 2026 Week 2: August 24 – August 27, 2026 (Virtual continuation) Qingshan Village, Hangzhou, China Week 1: July 3 – July 9, 2026 Week 2: August 24 – August 27, 2026 (Virtual continuation) Additional Components Regional networking events (dates to be announced) Final Summit: December 18, 2026 What the Enlight Fellowship Offers 1. Leadership and Entrepreneurship Training Participants engage in hands-on workshops covering: Business development and strategy Financial literacy and venture sustainability Entrepreneurial leadership and innovation 2. Financial Tools and Resources Access to practical frameworks for managing and scaling ventures Guidance from financial and business experts Support tailored to early-stage social enterprises 3. Basecamps: Community Impact Initiatives Fellows will: Design and lead a one-day workshop in their local community Receive training and a stipend to execute their Basecamp Strengthen local entrepreneurial ecosystems 4. Pitch Training and Global Exposure Develop a compelling pitch for your venture Present at a global Summit to: Investors Mentors Community leaders 5. Mentorship and Expert Guidance Fellows learn directly from experienced professionals, including: Entrepreneurs Industry experts Leadership coaches 6. Global Network and Alumni Support Join a lifelong community of changemakers Access ongoing mentorship and collaboration opportunities Weekly Commitment and Structure Participants should be prepared for an engaging and structured schedule: Weekly Workshops: Held Tuesday to Thursday Two available time slots: 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM MT 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM MT Weekly Commitment: 8–10 hours Workshops Mentorship sessions Community-building activities Project planning and execution Eligibility Criteria The Enlight Fellowship seeks passionate and driven individuals committed to improving youth mental wellbeing. Who You Are A leader working on solutions for youth mental health challenges Committed to creating scalable and meaningful impact Possess an entrepreneurial mindset and willingness to grow Your Venture Stage Operating for 1–5 years Fewer than 10 employees Demonstrated traction: Active users or customers Measurable results Some funding (grants, revenue, or investments) Commitment Requirements Full participation in the 16-week program Attendance at the mandatory in-person immersive experience Active engagement in all virtual sessions and activities Location Open to applicants worldwide Special focus on leaders addressing youth mental wellbeing in China and globally Age Requirement Applicants must be 18 years or older Application Process Step 1: Submit Application Complete the online application (approximately 30 minutes) Submit before the priority deadline for early consideration Step 2: Application Review Evaluated by the Admissions Committee Focus on innovation, scalability, and impact Step 3: Interview Shortlisted candidates attend a 20-minute virtual interview Step 4: Final Selection Results announced by July 20, 2026 Selected fellows confirm participation APPLY HERE Why You Should Apply for the Enlight Fellowship This fellowship is an exceptional opportunity for social entrepreneurs who want to: Scale their mental health initiatives globally Gain access to world-class mentorship and training Build a powerful international network Strengthen their leadership and entrepreneurial skills Create meaningful, sustainable change in their communities The Enlight Fellowship goes beyond traditional programs by combining leadership development, real-world application, and global collaboration. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . title: Richard J. Margolis Award 2026: A Prestigious Opportunity for Nonfiction Journalists Driving Social Change
summary: The Richard J. Margolis Award stands as a distinguished recognition for emerging nonfiction writers dedicated to social-justice journalism. Designed to support developing journalists, this award provides both recognition and the invaluable opportunity to further meaningful storytelling that addresses critical societal issues. If you are a writer passionate about creating impactful nonfiction narratives and advancing public understanding of social values, this award could be a transformative step in your career. About the Richard J. Margolis Award The Richard J. Margolis Award celebrates excellence in nonfiction writing focused on social justice themes . It aims to empower writers who are committed to producing thoughtful, well-researched journalism that highlights important social concerns. Each year, one recipient is selected and announced in November , followed by a residency at Blue Mountain Lake in the subsequent summer or fall. This residency provides a dedicated environment for writers to focus on their work and further develop their projects. Application Timeline and Deadline Applications are accepted year-round To be considered for the current year’s award, submissions must be received by July 1 Applications submitted after July 1 will automatically be considered for the following year What the Award Offers Winning the Richard J. Margolis Award provides: Recognition as an emerging voice in social-justice journalism A prestigious writing residency at Blue Mountain Lake Increased visibility within the literary and journalism communities Opportunities for career advancement and networking Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following materials: 1. Cover Letter Clearly state your interest in the award Highlight your qualifications and experience 2. Project Description Outline your current and upcoming work Explain what you plan to develop during the Blue Mountain residency 3. Biographical Note Provide a brief overview of your background , achievements, and writing journey 4. Writing Samples (2–3 pieces) All samples must meet the following criteria: Must be nonfiction only (no fiction or poetry) Maximum of 30 pages total At least one piece must not be memoir-based Can be published or unpublished Selection Criteria The Selection Committee evaluates applications based on: Quality of writing Journalistic skill and clarity Relevance to social justice themes Potential impact of the proposed project Applicants are encouraged to submit samples that demonstrate: Strong storytelling ability Investigative or analytical depth A clear commitment to meaningful social issues How to Submit Your Application Preferred Method: Email Submission Send all documents as PDF files Use clear file naming starting with your last name Example: Smith-coverletter.pdf , Smith-writingsample.pdf Submit to: award@margolis.com Alternative Method: Postal Mail Send materials to: Richard J. Margolis Award c/o Margolis Bloom 20 William Street, Suite 320 Wellesley, MA 02481 Note: Submitted materials will not be returned. Responsibilities of Award Recipients If selected, recipients are expected to: Acknowledge the award in: Online and print biographies Book acknowledgements Include a link to the official website in online profiles Provide updates on: Publications Career milestones Honors and achievements Updates can be sent via email or postal mail to the award committee. Why You Should Apply The Richard J. Margolis Award is more than just recognition—it is an opportunity to: Advance your nonfiction writing career Gain support for impact-driven journalism Join a network of writers committed to social change Dedicate time and space to a meaningful project For writers striving to make a difference through storytelling, this award offers both credibility and creative freedom. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Richard J. Margolis Award 2026: A Prestigious Opportunity for Nonfiction Journalists Driving Social Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Future 100 Growth Fund Mentor Registration: Empowering the Next Generation of Black Founders in the UK
summary: The Future 100 (F100) Growth Fund, a Sky-funded initiative, is a transformative programme designed to support Black founders across the United Kingdom. While the programme is widely recognized for providing financial backing and strategic growth opportunities to entrepreneurs, it also places strong emphasis on mentorship—an essential pillar for sustainable business success. To further strengthen its impact, the F100 Growth Fund is actively seeking experienced and passionate mentors who are ready to guide, inspire, and accelerate the journeys of emerging founders. If you have the expertise, network, and commitment to contribute meaningfully, this is a powerful opportunity to shape the future of entrepreneurship. About the F100 Growth Fund Programme The F100 Growth Fund is specifically tailored to uplift Black entrepreneurs by providing: Funding support of up to £15,000 to help scale their businesses Access to Sky’s supply chain , opening doors to large-scale business opportunities Investor networking opportunities , enabling founders to connect with potential funders and partners This initiative is not just about capital—it is about creating an ecosystem of growth, access, and long-term success. Why Mentorship Matters in This Programme Behind every successful entrepreneur is guidance, insight, and support. The F100 Growth Fund recognizes that mentorship can significantly accelerate business growth by: Helping founders avoid common pitfalls Offering strategic direction and decision-making support Expanding access to industry networks Providing real-world insights from experienced professionals Mentors play a critical role in shaping confident, capable, and scalable businesses. Who Should Apply as a Mentor? The programme is looking for individuals who align with one or more of the following criteria: 1. Passion for Developing Future Entrepreneurs A genuine commitment to nurturing and guiding the next generation Enthusiasm for contributing to entrepreneurial growth and innovation 2. Willingness to Share Knowledge and Networks Open to offering insights, advice, and connections Interested in actively contributing to the mentee’s development 3. Experience in Technology or Digitally Enabled Businesses Background in launching or scaling tech-driven ventures Familiarity with digital transformation, platforms, or innovation 4. Fundraising Expertise Proven experience in raising capital through: Angel investors Venture capital Seed funding Loan financing This experience is invaluable in helping founders navigate funding challenges. Mentor Commitment The programme is designed to be impactful yet manageable for busy professionals. Mentors are expected to: Commit at least 1 hour per month Engage in meaningful conversations with founders Provide actionable insights and guidance This flexible commitment ensures that mentors can contribute effectively without overwhelming their schedules. Benefits of Becoming an F100 Mentor Participating as a mentor offers both personal and professional rewards: Opportunity to shape emerging businesses Expand your professional network within the entrepreneurial ecosystem Gain exposure to innovative ideas and fresh perspectives Contribute to diversity and inclusion in business Enhance leadership and coaching skills Mentorship is a two-way exchange—while founders gain guidance, mentors gain renewed perspectives and purpose. How to Get Involved If you meet the criteria and are ready to contribute your time and expertise, the F100 Growth Fund welcomes your application. Ensure you can commit to at least one hour monthly Reflect on how your experience aligns with the programme’s needs Be prepared to actively support and guide founders Deadline: Not specified (applications may be considered on a rolling basis—early application is recommended) Final Thoughts The Future 100 Growth Fund is more than a funding initiative—it is a movement toward equitable opportunity and long-term entrepreneurial success. By becoming a mentor, you are not just advising a founder; you are helping build the future of business leadership in the UK. If you are ready to make a meaningful impact, this is your chance to step in and lead from the front. For more information about this opportunity, click here . Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . title: IAU Mini-Grant 2026: Funding Opportunity to Empower Women and Girls in Astronomy and STEM
summary: The International Astronomical Union (IAU) is now accepting applications for its Mini-Grant program, supporting innovative, community-based projects that promote inclusion, leadership, and participation of women and girls in astronomy and STEM. This initiative is a valuable opportunity for organizations and individuals working to close gender gaps in science and create more inclusive pathways into astronomy and related fields. About the IAU Mini-Grant The IAU Mini-Grant is designed to fund impactful, grassroots projects that encourage women and girls to engage with astronomy and STEM disciplines. The program focuses on initiatives that build leadership skills, expand access, and foster long-term participation in scientific communities. Projects should demonstrate creativity, community engagement, and a clear plan for impact. What the Grant Supports Selected proposals will receive funding to implement projects that: Promote inclusion and diversity in astronomy and STEM Empower women and girls through education and mentorship Strengthen community-based science initiatives Encourage leadership and participation in scientific fields Why This Opportunity Matters Despite growing interest in STEM, women and girls remain underrepresented in many scientific disciplines, including astronomy. This grant aims to address that gap by supporting initiatives that create opportunities, inspire participation, and build inclusive scientific communities. Who Can Apply The grant is open to applicants from the North American region who are working on projects aligned with the program’s goals. Eligible applicants may include: Nonprofit organizations and community groups Educators and academic institutions STEM advocates and project leaders Individuals or teams leading community-based initiatives Application Deadline All applications must be submitted by: May 31, 2026 Late submissions may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to apply early. How to Apply Interested applicants can learn more and submit their proposals here. For more Grant opportunities, Visit OFY here.
